methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $20M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $125M - $500M.

data.narrative:
Rune Technologies is a Series A-stage defense and security company founded by David Tuttle and Peter Goldsborough that has raised $20M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 10 total companies—2 public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms—all operating in the Defense & Security sector at equivalent or proximate stages.

Two triangulation methods were applied. The stage-progression approach uses the $20M total raised as a proxy baseline and applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A advancement, implying a $40M valuation. The peer-median method examines four same-sector, same-stage comparables: Stark Defence ($500M), Vatn Systems ($300M), Ares Industries ($150M), and NODA AI ($125M). This cohort yields a median valuation of $225M with a range spanning $125M to $500M. The wide dispersion reflects material differences in revenue traction, customer concentration, or technology maturity across the peer set.

Synthesizing both methods produces a triangulated valuation range of $34M (low) to $259M (high), with a median of $132M. The gap between the stage-progression floor and peer median reflects uncertainty around whether Rune's commercial progress and defensibility align with the higher-valued peer benchmarks or fall closer to the floor set by capital deployed. Key sensitivities include customer acquisition evidence, gross margins, and U.S. defense procurement pathway validation—none of which are quantified in available data.
